name: Credit.Org - Henderson Branch
description_short: Nonprofit credit counseling and HUD-approved housing advisor offering free debt relief, credit building, and homeownership guidance since 1974.
description_long:
Credit.org is a national nonprofit organization founded in 1974 that provides free financial counseling and education to consumers seeking debt relief, credit improvement, and housing stability. The organization operates as a HUD-approved housing counselor and appears to be affiliated with or part of a network delivering comprehensive financial guidance across multiple service areas.

The organization offers a broad range of free or low-cost services including debt management planning, credit score counseling, credit report review, reverse mortgage education and required counseling, pre-purchase homebuying education, foreclosure prevention assistance, disaster recovery financial planning, rental readiness coaching, and bankruptcy option exploration. They also provide structured financial education through their FIT Academy with self-paced courses, live interactive classes, budgeting calculators, and webinar offerings covering topics from basic budgeting to mortgage readiness.

Credit.org distinguishes itself through significant scale and longevity: the organization reports delivering $2.2 billion in homeowner support, completing over 5.2 million counseling sessions, and engaging 87,000+ online education participants. They employ certified nonprofit counselors, maintain partnerships with national and community organizations, and offer services in both English and Spanish. The three-step model (free session, personalized plan, goal achievement support) positions counseling as accessible and judgment-free, with specific appointment scheduling for distinct financial needs.

As a nonprofit counseling organization, Credit.org's primary limitation is that it provides guidance rather than direct financial products or debt settlement. Consumers seeking immediate debt reduction through settlement negotiations, personal loans, or credit repair services would need to look elsewhere. The organization's value proposition centers on education, planning, and connecting clients to appropriate resources rather than providing loans or direct debt payoff mechanisms.
pros: Completely free initial counseling session with no pressure or judgment
HUD-approved housing counseling with certified nonprofit counselors
Reverse mortgage counseling covering both HECM and Non-HECM products
Comprehensive FIT Academy with self-paced courses, live classes, and financial calculators
Bilingual services available in English and Spanish
Established 50+ year track record with $2.2B in homeowner support delivered
Specialized disaster recovery financial planning for post-emergency situations
cons: No direct debt settlement, consolidation loans, or credit repair services—counseling only
Generic website contact methodology may create delays for urgent financial situations
No indication of financial hardship grants or direct emergency assistance programs
Counseling plans are personalized guidance, not binding debt reduction agreements
Limited information about average timeline to see tangible financial results
best_for: Homebuyers seeking pre-purchase education, reverse mortgage counseling, or foreclosure prevention
Individuals with rising debt seeking structured management plans and financial literacy
Renters and those facing housing instability needing rental readiness or emergency housing guidance
Consumers wanting free credit counseling and debt strategy without pressure to enroll in paid programs
diagnosis: Credit.org is best for individuals seeking free, unbiased financial education and counseling to understand their options—not for those needing immediate debt reduction or loans. The primary value is access to certified nonprofit counselors and structured learning resources; the main caveat is that counseling alone does not reduce debt balances or repair credit scores directly.
services: Free one-on-one debt relief and housing stability counseling sessions
Credit report review and dispute guidance
HUD-approved reverse mortgage counseling (HECM and Non-HECM)
Rental readiness and rental help appointments
Live expert-led financial wellness classes and webinars
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 871 Coronado Center Dr, Suite 200, Henderson, NV 89052
